content: Read plan.md. Execute under strict constraints: (1) use context7 MCP for all framework/API info, no web search; (2) implement ONE use case at a time — write clean production TypeScript, self-test compilation, then STOP and give Postman steps + expected response, and wait for explicit 'Okay, proceed to the next USECASE' before continuing. Production-grade, secure, cleanly typed; no placeholders/TODOs; graceful DB/network/API error handling; must not break dev-server bootstrap or Vendure core config. Acknowledge, read plan.md, begin with base plugin structure for UC1.

Acknowledged all constraints (context7-only, one-use-case-at-a-time, production quality). Read plan.md: 8 use cases for a PayPal Vendure plugin with 4 internal modules (Payment Handler, Subscription Module, Reporting Module, Fulfillment Hook), OAuth2 client-credentials auth via env vars. Studied existing Stripe plugin for conventions and resolved PayPal Server SDK via context7 (/paypal/paypal-typescript-server-sdk). Fetched create-order/capture API. Installed @paypal/paypal-server-sdk@2.4.0 as optional peer + devDep (used --legacy-peer-deps due to a pre-existing harden-plugin/@apollo/server peer conflict). Built UC1 base plugin under packages/payments-plugin/src/paypal/: constants.ts, types.ts (PayPalPluginOptions), paypal-utils.ts (currency minor-unit<->decimal conversion), paypal-client.ts (SDK Client wrapper, OAuth2), paypal.service.ts (createOrder CAPTURE intent + captureOrder, graceful ApiError handling), paypal.handler.ts (PaymentMethodHandler: createPayment captures -> Settled), paypal.resolver.ts (createPayPalOrder shop mutation), paypal.plugin.ts, index.ts. Wired into dev-config.ts guarded by PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID/SECRET env presence so bootstrap never breaks. Self-test: tsc --noEmit EXIT=0, npm run build EXIT=0, package/paypal/ emitted, import resolves. Provided full Postman steps (add item, set customer/shipping, transition to ArrangingPayment, createPayPalOrder -> approveUrl, approve in browser, addPaymentToOrder with metadata.paypalOrderId -> PaymentSettled). Stopped for UC1 verification.

createPayPalOrder works and returns a valid approveUrl, but when the sandbox buyer approves it spins forever because there is no return/cancel URL configured. Fix the handler to include return_url and cancel_url (localhost is fine for sandbox). After the fix, confirm capture still works via addPaymentToOrder. Do not proceed to UC2; fix UC1 only.

Diagnosed: returnUrl/cancelUrl were undefined (dev-config didn't set them), so PayPal's hosted approval flow had no redirect target and spun. Fix: service.createOrder now always sends returnUrl/cancelUrl via a new resolveRedirectUrls() helper; added DEFAULT_RETURN_URL/DEFAULT_CANCEL_URL (localhost) fallbacks in constants.ts with a one-time warning when used; set explicit localhost URLs in dev-config PayPalPlugin.init(). The capture path (captureOrder/createPayment) was untouched. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0, confirmed fallback + unchanged capture in compiled JS. Gave retest steps (restart server; approval now redirects to localhost return URL; addPaymentToOrder still settles). Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE and remember only use context7

UC2 (Authorize-then-Capture). Fetched authorizeOrder + captureAuthorizedPayment + AuthorizationStatus from context7. While working, discovered the repo state had shifted: UC1 was committed; the package's untracked tsconfig.json/tsconfig.build.json had been cleaned (recreated them); stripe/mollie/braintree and src/index.ts were removed leaving only src/paypal, which made tsc flatten the build output and break the /package/paypal import path — fixed by pinning rootDir:'./src'. Also noted the root package.json is invalid JSON but that's committed in HEAD and tolerated by the runtime, so left it alone. Implemented UC2: added an 'intent' arg (capture|authorize) to the handler; service.createOrder reads configured intent; added authorizeOrder (-> Authorized, stores authorizationId) and captureAuthorization (captureAuthorizedPayment finalCapture:true); handler.createPayment branches to authorize, settlePayment captures the authorization. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0, layout restored to package/paypal/. Gave Postman steps (set method intent=authorize; createPayPalOrder; approve; addPaymentToOrder -> PaymentAuthorized; admin settlePayment -> PaymentSettled). Stopped.

The dev server won't start after UC2: dev-config.ts:24 Cannot find module '@vendure/sentry-plugin'; dev-config.ts:87 Type '"api-key"' is not assignable to '"cookie"|"bearer"'. Fix so the server starts. Do not touch any UC2 logic.

Both errors were in dev-config.ts (user edits), unrelated to UC2. SentryPlugin was imported but never used (its conditional block had been removed) and sentry-plugin had no built output -> removed the unused import. 'api-key' isn't a valid core tokenMethod -> reverted to ['bearer','cookie']. Verified dev-config.ts has 0 type errors. Noted pre-existing errors in core/src api-key.resolver.ts (a half-wired core feature) which don't block startup since the dev server consumes @vendure/core from built dist. No PayPal/UC2 logic touched.

UC2 testing fails consistently across browsers/sessions: 'Failed to capture PayPal order 0AK48067XW1884334 (PayPal error 422)'. The payment method intent is set to authorize. Investigate and fix. Don't move to UC3; only fix this.

Root cause: createPayment chose capture-vs-authorize from the config arg (args.intent), but the PayPal order's intent is fixed at creation; these can diverge (multiple methods, config drift, or a method saved before the arg existed -> argsArrayToHash omits it with no default), and capturing an AUTHORIZE order yields 422 ACTION_DOES_NOT_MATCH_INTENT. Fix (verified via context7 getOrder + Order.intent): createPayment now reads the PayPal order's actual intent via OrdersController.getOrder and branches on that; settlePayment branches on whether the payment recorded an authorizationId (fails safe if missing rather than settling without capturing). The 'intent' config arg still governs order creation. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0, getOrderIntent wired in handler+service. Gave retest steps. Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC3 (Payment Cancellation/Void). Fetched PaymentsController.voidPayment from context7 (returns PaymentAuthorization|null; success often 204 empty body). Maps to handler.cancelPayment (Admin cancelPayment mutation). Added service.voidAuthorization (success inferred from non-throwing 2xx) + VoidAuthorizationResult; handler.cancelPayment reads authorizationId from metadata, voids, returns success with metadata {voided:true, authorizationStatus}; 422 (already captured) caught and returned as clean failure; guards missing authorizationId. Confirmed Admin schema: cancelPayment(id:ID!):CancelPaymentResult = Payment|CancelPaymentError|PaymentStateTransitionError. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0, cancelPayment->voidAuthorization->voidPayment wired. Gave Postman steps (authorize but don't settle; cancelPayment -> Cancelled, authorizationStatus VOIDED; failure: settle-then-cancel -> PaymentStateTransitionError). Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC4 (Full Refund). Fetched refundCapturedPayment from context7 (no body=full refund, body with amount=partial; Refund id/status). Maps to handler.createRefund (Admin refundOrder mutation). Added service.refundCapture(captureId, amount?) + RefundCaptureResult; handler.createRefund reads captureId from metadata, maps RefundStatus->RefundState (COMPLETED->Settled, PENDING->Pending, FAILED/CANCELLED->Failed), catches errors as Failed refund. Detects full-vs-partial by comparing requested amount to payment.amount (full omits amount per plan). Flagged transparently that the cohesive method inherently also supports partial (UC5). Confirmed Admin refundOrder(input:RefundOrderInput!) with amount:Money + paymentId:ID!. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0. Gave Postman full-refund steps + NothingToRefundError failure. Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC5 (Partial Refund). No new code required — it's the partial branch of the createRefund method from UC4. Verified in the build: body: amount ? { amount } : {} in service, and handler sends explicit toPayPalAmount(amount,currency) when amount < payment.amount; multiple partials each create an independent PayPal refund with no idempotency collision; Vendure caps cumulative at payment.amount (RefundAmountError). Confirmed RefundOrderResult union error types. Provided Postman steps for two sequential partial refunds + the over-refund cap failure. Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC6 (Subscription Billing) — large module. Fetched Subscriptions API from context7 (billing-plan CRUD, createSubscription, captureSubscription, cancel, AuthorizationStatus). Asked two scoping questions; user chose: build the FULL module now, and scheduler role = status sync / failure detection. Confirmed exact SDK shapes via local .d.ts introspection. Key findings: SDK v2.4.0 has NO CatalogsController (so plans require a merchant-supplied PROD- product id) and the Subscription model drops the 'status' field (read from raw response body). Built subscription/ module: PayPalBillingPlan + PayPalSubscription entities; PayPalSubscriptionService (plan create/activate/deactivate/update via patchBillingPlan + updateBillingPlanPricingSchemes; subscription create/sync/cancel; retryPayment via captureSubscription of outstanding balance; syncActiveSubscriptions); admin + shop resolvers; GraphQL extensions; an hourly ScheduledTask (paypalSubscriptionSyncTask) registered via schedulerOptions.tasks. Extended PayPalClient with SubscriptionsController; made PayPalService.getClient() shared. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0, all 8 module files emitted. Gave Postman steps (create+activate plan with PROD- id; shop createPayPalSubscription -> approveUrl; approve; activate; admin list/sync/retry/cancel). Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC7 (Transaction Reporting). Fetched TransactionSearchController searchTransactions + searchBalances from context7; confirmed SearchResponse/TransactionInformation/BalancesResponse/BalanceInformation shapes locally. Built reporting/ module (no entities, live data): PayPalReportingService.searchTransactions (auto-splits >31-day ranges into windows and stitches, paginating each; amounts to integer minor units, negative for refunds) + getBalances; admin resolver + schema (payPalTransactions, payPalBalances). Extended PayPalClient with TransactionSearchController; merged into the plugin's adminApiExtensions. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0. Gave Postman steps (transaction search, status filter, >31-day stitch test, balances) + notes (requires Transaction Search feature enabled on the PayPal app; up to 3-hour delay; reconciliation-only). Stopped.
Okay, proceed to the next USECASE. only use context7

UC8 (Order Shipment Tracking) — final. Fetched OrdersController.createOrderTracking + OrderTrackerRequest + ShipmentCarrier from context7. Confirmed Vendure FulfillmentStateTransitionEvent shape, fulfillment->order resolution (getFulfillmentLines -> OrderLine.order), Fulfillment.trackingCode/method, and EventBus.ofType subscription pattern. Built fulfillment/paypal-fulfillment.service.ts (no entities/endpoints): on bootstrap subscribes to FulfillmentStateTransitionEvent; when toState=Shipped, resolves the order(s), finds the Settled PayPal payment (metadata.paypalOrderId/captureId), and pushes tracking via createOrderTracking (captureId, trackingNumber, carrier OTHER + carrierNameOther=method, notifyPayer:true). Fire-and-forget with error isolation so tracking failures never disrupt fulfillment. Registered as plugin provider. Self-test: tsc EXIT=0, build EXIT=0. Confirmed manual-fulfillment handler code/args. Gave Postman steps (addFulfillmentToOrder with method+trackingCode; transitionFulfillmentToState Shipped fires the async hook; verify via server logs + PayPal sandbox dashboard). All 8 use cases now complete; summarized the table. Stopped for final verification.

Created this JSONL transcript of the conversation and reported its path.
